description_long: This house features a steeply pitched, shingled gable roof with a prominent dormer window on one side, evoking a medieval or Tudor-inspired architectural style. The exterior walls are constructed from a combination of stone at the base and timber framing with wooden panels above, giving it a rustic, old-world charm. Multiple windows with crossbars are symmetrically arranged on the upper and lower floors, framed by exposed wooden beams. The main entryway is marked by a double wooden door set into a stone arch, accessed by a small set of stone steps. Decorative wooden brackets and exposed rafters add to the visual complexity of the roofline. The overall structure is compact and vertically oriented, with two full stories and a lofted attic space.
description: A two-story, timber-framed house with a steep gable roof, stone base, and decorative wooden details. Features include a dormer window, symmetrical crossbar windows, and a prominent arched entryway.
description_view_0: Front and left side view showing the main arched entryway, stone steps, and prominent dormer window above.
description_view_1: Rear and left side view highlighting the dormer window and the timber-framed upper story, with no visible entryway.
description_view_2: Front and right side view displaying the main door, stone base, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_3: Rear and right side view focusing on the large expanse of the shingled roof and multiple windows, with the entryway partially visible.
attribute_entryway_design: Arched double wooden door with stone steps, framed by timber and stone, no porch or columns.
